R1_LODO_donor_level_ASSUMPTION_FAITHFUL.result: VACUOUS. At n=4 donors the distribution-free UCB on miscoverage is 0.74/0.64/0.59 (Hoeffding) vs targets 0.20/0.10/0.05 — no certificate. This is the HONEST core: the assumption-faithful granularity for the flagged donor shift has too few exchangeable units to certify.

Reported as false-precision contrast, NOT a claimed guarantee.

R3_Norman_per_item_recalibrated_EXCHANGE.exchangeable_unit: Norman K562 single-gene perturbation (n=105; 50/50 split seed 20260711)

primary_alpha_0.10.interpretation: exchangeable by random split -> RCPS certificate VALID & non-vacuous.

R3_Norman_per_item_recalibrated_EXCHANGE.result: VALID & NON-VACUOUS. RCPS certifies half-width lambda_hat=0.527 at 90% (UCB 0.099<=0.10); realized held-out-test coverage 0.943. Certificates at 80/90/95 all hold with realized test coverage 0.887/0.943/0.943.

check: TRACK A2 — Distribution-free RISK CONTROL (RCPS) on the committed conformal machinery + conformal-transfer restatement

comparison_naive_vs_riskcontrol.finding: In the COMMITTED LODO donor regime (R1, the assumption-faithful granularity), RCPS is VACUOUS at n=4 — it does NOT beat the naive fold-spread summary there. RCPS's genuine contribution is (a) making the exchangeability price EXPLICIT and QUANTIFIED (vacuous at n=4 donors; certifiable only at n=105 exchangeable items), and (b) a deployable certificate in the per-assay recalibrated regime (R3). It is a real but SCOPED methodological improvement, not a strict upgrade of the LODO headline.
comparison_naive_vs_riskcontrol.naive_LODO_empirical_statement: committed conformal reports fold_mean +/- fold_std (90% -> 0.8955 +/- 0.0055) and 'fold_interval contains nominal' — an empirical observation with a donor-spread uncertainty band.
comparison_naive_vs_riskcontrol.riskcontrol_statement: RCPS attempts a finite-sample, distribution-free HIGH-PROBABILITY certificate on the miscoverage risk.

conformal_transfer_result_restated.claim: Frozen T1D conformal quantiles fail cross-assay on Norman K562; RECALIBRATING the identical split-conformal method on Norman's own calibration split restores coverage.
conformal_transfer_result_restated.message_for_writeup: The conformal GUARANTEE generalizes across assays under exchangeability — you must recalibrate per assay, and when you do (RCPS on the recalibrated split) you get a deployable finite-sample certificate. The specific frozen quantiles are assay-bound (expected under distribution shift); the method is not.
conformal_transfer_result_restated.reproduction_check: frozen Norman residual mean/sd 0.6044/0.2951 and frozen coverage 0.0476/0.0762/0.1714 reproduced EXACTLY from cache — the same numbers RCPS R3 is built on.

guarantee_statement: RCPS certificate: with probability >= 1-delta over the calibration draw, the deployed interval pred +/- lambda_hat has true miscoverage R(lambda_hat) <= alpha ON A POPULATION EXCHANGEABLE WITH THE CALIBRATION UNITS. We set alpha=delta=0.10 (certify >=90% coverage at 90% confidence). The guarantee is EXACTLY AS STRONG AS its exchangeability precondition: it controls calibration-SAMPLING error, not distribution shift. Under donor-blocked LODO the exchangeable unit is the DONOR (n=4); at that assumption-faithful granularity the distribution-free UCB is VACUOUS. Under per-assay recalibration (Norman K562, random calib/test split of the same 105 perturbations) exchangeability holds BY CONSTRUCTION and the certificate is valid and non-vacuous.
honest_verdict: MARGINAL / SCOPED-POSITIVE. RCPS is a genuine distribution-free risk-control layer and delivers a VALID, non-vacuous finite-sample certificate in the per-assay recalibrated regime (Norman K562, n=105 exchangeable items: lambda_hat=0.527 certifies <=10% miscoverage at 90% confidence, realized held-out coverage 0.943). BUT in the committed donor-blocked LODO setting, applied at the assumption-FAITHFUL granularity (held-out donor, n=4), the distribution-free UCB is VACUOUS (0.74/0.64/0.59 vs targets 0.20/0.10/0.05) — RCPS cannot certify what the naive fold-spread statement already summarizes, because there are only 4 exchangeable donor units. The row-level version that looks tight (n=58,558) rests on the exact exchangeability the project flags as VIOLATED, so it is false precision, not a guarantee. Verdict: the naive empirical fold-spread statement was ADEQUATE for the LODO headline; RCPS's real value is elsewhere — it quantifies the exchangeability price honestly and gives a deployable per-assay certificate. A real methods extension, honestly scoped, NOT a strict improvement of the committed LODO coverage claim.
honesty_contract: Design + method choice + success criterion pre-registered and hash-frozen (prereg 16aca796...) BEFORE scoring. Scores the COMMITTED pipeline (frozen conformal quantiles + frozen Marson base predictor); no surrogate. R1/R2 from committed conformal_lodo_receipt.json; R3 from cached norman_group_stats.npz + frozen_marson_calibration.joblib. No 16GB h5mu re-stream.
reproducibility: python -c reproduces R1/R2 from data/gold/conformal_lodo_receipt.json and R3 from data/interim/norman_group_stats.npz + frozen_marson_calibration.joblib. Module: src/trustlayer/risk_control.py.
risk_control_method: RCPS (Risk-Controlling Prediction Sets; Bates, Angelopoulos, Lei, Malik, Jordan, JACM 2021)
why_RCPS_over_LTT: Miscoverage R(lambda)=P(|y-pred|>lambda) is a single monotone-decreasing risk over NESTED prediction sets. RCPS is the assumption-matched instrument (scan lambda, stop at smallest lambda whose finite-sample UCB on the risk <= alpha). LTT would certify the same guarantee here via FWER multiple-testing over a lambda family — strictly not tighter for one monotone parameter.
